channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: Whole lotta love for Led Zeppelin star given plaque
item.description: Led Zeppelin superstar Robert Plant left shoppers dazed and confused when he was spotted crate digging in the world's oldest record store.

item.title: Anna Wintour’s Vogue cover is more than a cameo – it’s a power play
item.description: In the world of magazines, when someone announces they’re leaving a job, their colleagues will traditionally present them with their own personalised mock-up of the magazine’s front cover.

item.title: Is space exploration worth the money and effort? | Letters
item.description: Zoe Williams speaks for many of us when she notes that the US space mission is pointless (Let’s stop going into space.

item.title: I took up the ukulele after a chance celebrity encounter. It’s brought me more happiness than falling in love | Melanie Tait
item.description: Avowed ukulele lover George Harrison once wrote in a note to a friend “Everyone I know who is into the ukulele is ‘crackers’.

item.title: Gout and Kennedy renew rivalry, Hull eyes history as Australian athletics puts its best on show
item.description: An array of exotic, well-trimmed dogs will parade around Sydney Olympic Park this weekend as part of the Royal Easter Show.

item.title: Canada's Mark Carney 'so proud' of astronauts in call to Artemis II
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney told the astronauts of the Artemis II mission that Canadians 'couldn't be more proud' of them.

item.title: The best water flossers in the UK, tested for that dentist-clean feeling
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals her true identity
item.description: The bestselling thriller writer known as Freida McFadden has revealed her real identity, ending years of speculation about the author behind a string of hugely popular novels.

item.title: Britain's 'most beautiful coin' sold for £110,000
item.description: A coin considered the "most beautiful" ever minted in Britain has sold at auction for £110,000.

item.title: Dream-pop at its most divine: Cocteau Twins’ 20 greatest songs – ranked!
item.description: At first, Cocteau Twins gave every impression of being a goth band: check out Wax and Wane’s Banshees-esque ambience – the guitar is very John McGeoch – flanged bass and drum machine.

item.title: Most Hungarians want better relations with EU after years of Orbán Brussels-bashing, poll finds
item.description: Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win?.

item.title: Belle and Sebastian review – joyful anniversary tour makes debut album brighter than ever
item.description: It’s a double 30th anniversary for Belle and Sebastian, whose first two albums, Tigermilk and If You’re Feeling Sinister, both came out in 1996.

item.title: Best-selling The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals true identity
item.description: One of the biggest mysteries in publishing has been solved, after the best-selling US author known to millions of readers as Freida McFadden revealed her true identity.

item.title: Live: Festival of footy kicks off with Crows–Blues sellout in Adelaide
item.description: The fourth edition of AFL's Gather Round gets underway with the Adelaide Crows hosting Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval.

item.title: Big Mistakes review – Schitt’s Creek creator Dan Levy excels in new cringe comedy
item.description: There are, broadly speaking, two types of television shows: the ones that make stars and the ones made by stars.

item.title: Beloved Queensland pub faces demolition for resort-style development
item.description: A historic pub in Mackay will be demolished if an application for a resort-style development on the site is approved.

item.title: You Are the Führer’s Unrequited Love by Jean-Noël Orengo review – Hitler, Speer and beyond
item.description: In April 1975, Simon Wiesenthal, the Holocaust survivor whose efforts to track down Nazi war criminals had earned him the title of “Nazi hunter”, wrote a letter to Albert Speer, the Nazi war criminal.

item.title: Artemis crew returning to Earth with 'all the good stuff' from Moon discoveries
item.description: The Artemis II crew said they have "many more pictures" and "many more stories" to share with the world as they prepare to return to Earth.

item.title: 'Growing up with deaf parents inspired my new comedy show'
item.description: When Ray Bradshaw was growing up, he never thought there was anything unusual about the fact he could hear while his parents were deaf.

item.title: Artemis II is 'inspiring' a whole generation
item.description: Susan Charlesworth has a rather rare and unique job that involves preparing people for something very few humans have ever expierenced - travelling into space.

item.title: Tigers searching for third straight win to end eight-year streak
item.description: The Wests Tigers themselves might not be talking about it, but every sicko you know of a black, white and gold persuasion certainly is.

item.title: You, Me & Tuscany review – slick romcom offers solidly charming getaway
item.description: Halle Bailey is Anna, hopelessly navigating life after the death of her mother, torn between the worlds of adult responsibility and inner child whimsy.

item.title: ‘My background cringes me out’: Jack Whitehall on poshness, comedy and his lockdown romance
item.description: ‘After every tour, I hate the sound of my voice,’ the actor and comedian says.

item.title: Hundreds more PSOs at Melbourne stations if Liberals win state election
item.description: The Victorian Opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins the state election in November.

item.title: $10m horse will never race, but owner insists she is worth every cent
item.description: The first filly out of champion mare Winx sold at auction for a world record $10 million in 2024.

item.title: Dan Rath: Help Me Please review – you won’t find another standup with more jokes a minute
item.description: Don’t come to Dan Rath’s new show expecting something coherent.

item.title: Comedian Grace Jarvis on most bizarre encounter working in sex shop
item.description: But look a little closer, and you'll see why Rolling Stone last year dubbed this rising Queensland comic a must-see at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival.

item.title: Early season brilliance puts Gawn on track for record All-Australian nod
item.description: The first month of the 2026 AFL season has come and gone, and the ladder is finally beginning to take shape.

item.title: Barcelona on the brink after red card and Alvarez stunner sparks Atlético win
item.description: When the final whistle went, the man in black disappeared out of sight and set off running up the tunnel.

item.title: 'It's all good': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia after visa reinstated
item.description: A British man who sexually abused a nine-year-old girl has had his Australian permanent visa reinstated by a tribunal, after it was automatically cancelled following his conviction.

item.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
item.description: With minutes before a self-imposed deadline – and an unprecedented threat to wipe out Iran's "civilisation" - US President Donald Trump announced that a two-week ceasefire had been agreed to halt the war.

item.title: Egg-laying hens in cages set for better living conditions under new laws
item.description: The Victorian government is introducing new regulations for the care and management of poultry, including changes to chicken cages.

item.title: Family still waits to know how their loved one died, one year on
item.description: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain v Liverpool: Champions League quarter-final, first leg – live
item.description: double quotation markWhile Arsenal were busy Arsenaling their way to a worthy but dull Bigger Cup quarter-final first leg win at Sporting on Tuesday, the players of Real Madrid and Bayern Munich released the collective handbrake and performed many high-speed donuts, wheel spins and Rockfords as a pleasing counterpoint to the careful mirror-signal-manoeuvring on display at Lisbon’s Estádio José Alvalade.

item.title: British novelist Gwendoline Riley wins a $175k Windham-Campbell prize
item.description: British novelist Gwendoline Riley is among eight writers set to receive $175,000 (£130,000) each in recognition of their life’s work.

item.title: Starmer says UK wants to help with opening Hormuz strait amid closure reports
item.description: The UK has a “job” to help reopen the strait of Hormuz, Keir Starmer has said, as Iranian reports said the key shipping route was closed again just hours after a supposed ceasefire.

item.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
item.description: Far-Flung Postcards is a weekly series in which NPR's international team shares moments from their lives and work around the world.

item.title: New light shed on who benefits most from weight-loss jabs
item.description: People who carry variations in two genes linked to appetite and digestion can lose more weight when taking drugs such as Wegovy and Mounjaro to treat obesity, research suggests.

item.title: An irresistible adventure activity for New Zealand visitors? Delivering the mail by boat
item.description: For a travel destination famous for offering the adrenaline rush of extreme sports, from bungee jumping to the parachute drop, it’s an unlikely tourist activity – but an irresistible one.

item.title: Are OnlyFans models the best way to explain the climate crisis?
item.description: Actor Megan Prescott has joined with Adam McKay in the hope that showing bite-size web videos of women undressing will persuade us to save the world.

item.title: What does the dark side of the moon sound like? Nasa’s sonifications are helping us imagine
item.description: Jaw-dropping dark-siding exploration aside, it’s the mundane details of the Artemis II mission that connect us with the four astronauts slingshotting their way around the moon and back.

item.title: Carnival celebrations at a Hungarian retirement home: János Bődey’s best photograph
item.description: When I saw these two elderly ladies dressed as bride and groom in a retirement home in Páty, near Budapest, my first thought was to wonder why they had chosen those particular costumes.

item.title: Euphoria stars hit red carpet at premiere of third - and possibly final - season
item.description: TV drama Euphoria has been the launchpad for the careers of some of Hollywood's hottest stars - and they have reunited on the red carpet for the premiere of the show's third and possibly final season.

item.title: Two for two? Stella prize winner Evelyn Araluen nominated again for second poetry collection
item.description: Evelyn Araluen has been shortlisted for the Stella prize for her second poetry collection The Rot, four years after she became the first ever poet to win the prize for Australian women and non-binary writers.

item.title: Abortion clinics are closing nationwide. Could urgent care help fill the gap?
item.description: Providing abortions was the last thing Shawn Brown thought she'd be doing when she opened an urgent care clinic in Marquette, a small port town on the remote shores of Michigan's Upper Peninsula.

item.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses
item.description: The offer of a free trip on public transport is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others use it for new experiences.

item.title: When is the best time to get your flu vaccine?
item.description: We usually have to wait until winter approaches before we see an increase in cases of influenza, or the flu.

item.title: Books changed my life, says Queen's first reading hero
item.description: Selina Brown pored over books as a child, enthralled at the wonders leaping from the pages.

item.title: Modern heroes and a ravaged Earth: reboot of 1950s space comic Dan Dare has liftoff
item.description: Sufferin’ satellites! The quintessential British space hero Dan Dare is back, 76 years after he first appeared in iconic comic magazine the Eagle.

item.title: Republican wins Georgia runoff election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
item.description: Clay Fuller supports the war in Iran.

item.title: Squid sightings spawn hope for fast species recovery in South Australia
item.description: Southern calamari has been spotted in parts of South Australia where it had vanished.

item.title: Arteta admits Arsenal had point to prove after ‘big moment in season’ with win at Sporting
item.description: Mikel Arteta acknowledged that Arsenal had a point to prove against Sporting after losing their last two matches and believes that Kai Havertz’s late winner could prove to be a “big moment” in their season.

item.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'
item.description: Australian seafood is set to become more expensive unless fuel supplies return to normal.

item.title: Will Democratic wins affect plans for Florida redistricting?
item.description: After Democrats flipped two seats in surprising legislative elections, Florida political insiders weigh whether Republican should risk redistricting later this month to win more House seats.

item.title: Live: Albanese talks energy security with China's premier
item.description: Anthony Albanese and Chinese Premier Li Qiang have discussed energy security ahead of the prime minister's dash to Singapore later this week.

item.title: Real Madrid 1-2 Bayern Munich: Champions League quarter-final first leg – live reaction
item.description: double quotation mark“This is why you guys come to these games,” Vincent Kompany had said, and here especially.

item.title: Troy the parrot home safe after Dublin Airport adventure
item.description: A parrot discovered at Dublin Airport has been safely reunited with its owner, thanks to the efforts of airport staff and the public.

item.title: Women’s Super League academy teams to play in third tier under new FA proposals
item.description: Major structural changes to the Women’s National League, including four Women’s Super League academy sides entering tier three of the pyramid from 2027 and the introduction of a mid-season split similar to that used in Scotland, have been proposed by the Football Association, the Guardian can reveal.

item.title: Charity cleared after false claims online over migrant welcome project
item.description: A refugee charity subjected to vicious social media attacks over a migrant welcome project in schools has been cleared of wrongdoing after watchdogs found allegations it encouraged pupils to send Valentine’s Day cards to asylum seekers were misleading and false.

item.title: Legendary Disney composer Alan Menken on winning Oscars, Razzies and his ‘filthy’ rock musical
item.description: In early 1991, the composer Alan Menken took a keyboard to St Vincent’s hospital in New York to visit his friend and creative partner, the lyricist Howard Ashman.

item.title: Max Chandler-Mather says Greens can use ‘progressive populism’ to win voters deserting major parties for One Nation
item.description: Max Chandler-Mather says the Greens can use “progressive economic populism” to win over Australians deserting the major parties for One Nation as the firebrand former MP accused the political class of thumbing its nose at the concerns of everyday voters.

item.title: ‘We’ll start a creche’: how the World Surf League is becoming family friendly for parents on tour | Kieran Pender
item.description: This year’s Rip Curl Pro at Bells Beach has felt different for Connor O’Leary.

item.title: 3D heart to let surgeons practise baby's vital heart operation
item.description: A couple say their "world will stop" on the day their baby goes into an operating theatre for life-saving open heart surgery.

item.title: Who needs looksmaxxing when you’ve got Catholicmaxxing? The TikTok trend making religion great again
item.description: What’s with all this maxxing business anyway? Oh, don’t concern yourself too much with the nomenclature.

item.title: Women in Northern Ireland welcome introduction of miscarriage leave
item.description: Northern Ireland has become the first part of the UK where a woman and her partner are entitled to two weeks of paid leave if they experience a miscarriage at any stage of pregnancy.

item.title: Neighbour plan to help food bank expand
item.description: Worcester Foodbank is set to expand after securing permission to take over a neighbouring unit.

item.title: JP Morgan reaches agreement with City airport for Canary Wharf’s tallest tower
item.description: JP Morgan Chase has reached agreement with London City airport to build one of Europe’s tallest office towers in the east of the capital.

item.title: ‘Even more beautiful than I imagined’: the nifty Japanese printing gadget uniting artists worldwide
item.description: Gabriella Marcella felt something in her brain click when she first used a risograph printer.

item.title: How to make better coffee – without spending a fortune
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Pet Shop Boys review – no hits? No problem on first night of a masterful obscurities run
item.description: ‘Tonight …” says Neil Tennant, with a suave pause, “no hits!” The crowd roars.

item.title: ‘Before I can stop her, my daughter is licking crumbs from the table’: my search for the perfect kids’ menu
item.description: We’re heading out for dinner.

item.title: Intel is going all-in on advanced chip packaging
item.description: Sixteen miles north of Albuquerque, in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, an Intel chip plant sits on more than 200 acres of land.

item.title: The Stranger review – lustrously beautiful and superbly realised modern take on the Camus classic
item.description: A heatstricken reverie of violence and mystery unfolds in this film, a numb ecstasy of the inexplicable, as experienced by a sensitive white European under the unbearable noonday sun.

item.title: Gibson jumps at chance to spearhead Australia against Great Britain
item.description: Talia Gibson says it was an "immediate yes" when asked to be part of Australia's Billie Jean King Cup team against Great Britain.

item.title: The Masters is a welcome oasis in golf’s fractious world, despite its stuffy foibles | Ewan Murray
item.description: It is easy to poke fun at the prissy traditions of the Masters.

item.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
item.description: In the second half of the 20th Century, it was the race to develop nuclear arms that occupied some of the finest minds in the US and the Soviet Union.

item.title: ‘Italy has the best benefits’: Milan takes on Dubai as home for the super-rich
item.description: Just over a month ago, Dubai was the obvious destination for wealthy Britons in search of a new home.

item.title: Cyclist clinging to reeds, plucked to safety in remote Snowy River rescue
item.description: A cyclist who tried to ride across the Snowy River at Suggan Buggan in remote far-east Gippsland has been plucked to safety in a dramatic overnight rescue.

item.title: Call for parents to teach online privacy like road safety
item.description: Parents should teach children online privacy as an essential life skill in the same way as stranger danger or road safety, the UK's data watchdog has said.

item.title: Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months
item.description: Boat wrecks still line parts of north Queensland's coast months after Cyclone Koji crossed between Bowen and Ayr.

item.title: Wireless Festival boss defends Kanye West booking and asks people to forgive rapper
item.description: The managing director of Wireless Festival has defended the decision to choose Kanye West as a headliner, encouraging people to offer the rapper "forgiveness".

item.title: Wireless festival says Kayne West still due to perform despite calls for ban
item.description: The promoter of Wireless festival has stood by the decision to have Kanye West perform at the event, despite an outcry over the rapper’s antisemitism and calls to cancel his appearance.

item.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths
item.description: First Nations voters have told elected officials about facing discrimination at polling booths when attempting to cast their vote in the SA First Nations Voice to Parliament vote.

item.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs
item.description: How much would you pay for someone to turn a projector on for you every night for a week? $100? $200 and a couple drinks?.

item.title: Live: Artemis II breaks distance record, Moon 'bright spot' named after commander's late wife
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, surpassing the record set by Apollo 13 in 1970 at 400,171 kilometres.

item.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
item.description: Albert Mazibuko, who sang in iconic South African group Ladysmith Black Mambazo for more than five decades, has died aged 77.

item.title: The FA Cup still has an important place. This weekend was proof
item.description: The soccer calendar has been particularly quirky this year.

item.title: Over-the-counter medication abortion? These researchers say it would be safe
item.description: Imagine that you're pregnant, a few weeks in, and you decide you want an abortion.

item.title: Is a new weight-loss drug making people fall out of love?
item.description: What are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?.

item.title: Championship, Chelsea v Tottenham in Women’s FA Cup, and more: football – live
item.description: The new Football Weekly has dropped.

item.title: Georgina Hayden’s quick and easy recipe for gochujang butter salmon | Quick and easy
item.description: The classic combination of soy sauce and honey salmon is a staple in our house, and works for kids and adults alike.

item.title: ‘All we can do now is pray they continue’: Maasai welcome the first rains but know that drought is far from over
item.description: Kampalei has lived for more than 50 of his 70 years with his family and animals in Oldonyonyokie, a hamlet in southern Kenya’s Kajiado county.

item.title: 'Really feeling the love' - Savannah Guthrie returns to NBC as search for mother goes on
item.description: US television presenter Savannah Guthrie has returned to NBC's Today show, telling her co-anchors it was "good to be back", as the search for her mother continues.

item.title: Spanish school emerges again as the superior model for Champions League success | Philipp Lahm
item.description: In Germany, coaches used to say: “Follow your opponent right into the loo!” That was the call to man-mark.

item.title: The Joy of Six: moments of unbridled joy in sport
item.description: The pain of failure, both professional and personal, is one we all know well.

item.title: ‘The original triple threat’: two exhibitions celebrate Marilyn Monroe as creative pioneer
item.description: Though often reduced to a sex symbol frozen in time, or a tragic figure at the centre of several scandals, Marilyn Monroe was something far more subversive, according to two exhibitions that will herald what has been nicknamed “the summer of Marilyn”.

item.title: Chinese AI helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision'
item.description: Analysts say images taken by a Chinese company could be helping Iran to target its opponents with extreme precision.

item.title: Commuter thanks stranger who restarted his heart
item.description: Spencer Stevenson nearly died on his way to work one morning in December, as he suddenly suffered a cardiac arrest after getting off his train.

item.title: Is it true that … more testosterone means more muscle?
item.description: It’s an increasingly popular idea: “boosting” testosterone with diet tweaks – increasing foods rich in zinc and magnesium – hoping to build muscle faster.

item.title: No Bluesfest? No worries as stars come out to wow Byron Easter crowds
item.description: Byron Bay's iconic Bluesfest event was cancelled with less than a month's notice, but many music lovers flocked to the area nonetheless.

item.title: Monday briefing: Can human-based space exploration still be meaningful?
item.description: Good morning.

item.title: From volcanic wilds to world-class art: 10 fun and fabulous reasons to visit France in 2026
item.description: You don’t need to venture too far into France to find its wow factor.

item.title: US sprint star Sha’Carri Richardson wins 2026 Stawell Gift off scratch in thrilling finish
item.description: American sprint queen Sha’Carri Richardson has lived up to her star status as she chased down the field off scratch to win the 2026 Stawell Gift in a thrilling finish.

item.title: Live: Eels enjoy fast start against Tigers in front of sold out crowd
item.description: Parramatta looks to bounce back after last week's mauling by the Panthers by taking on the in-form Wests Tigers on Easter Monday.

item.title: Three easy sausage dinners that don't involve mash and peas
item.description: Sausages are an easy and popular meal option but sometimes it's nice to mix things up.

item.title: Live: Gunston kicks behind in dying seconds to win epic over Cats
item.description: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston has kicked a behind in the dying seconds to defeat Geelong by one point in an Easter Monday classic.

item.title: Uluru's popular Field of Light installation extended for three years
item.description: A blanket of stars hovers atop the midnight blue sky on a cool desert evening in Central Australia.

item.title: Sha'Carri Richardson wins Stawell Gift from scratch
item.description: American sprint star Sha'Carri Richardson has become the third woman ever to win the famous Stawell Gift from scratch.

item.title: Cycling great avoids disqualification over train crossing to win Tour of Flanders
item.description: Tadej Pogačar has won the one-day Tour of Flanders for a record-equalling third time after the men's field was split by a train passing through a level crossing.

item.title: Aerial view of American sprinter Sha'Carri Richardson winning her Stawell Gift heat - video
item.description: USA sprint star Sha’Carri Richardson has cruised into the Stawell Gift semi-final, comfortably winning her heat on Saturday afternoon.

item.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to live with child's autism in a country with little help
item.description: Martha Ongwane looks adoringly at her bouncy, giggling four-year-old, unable to believe that just two years ago she had wanted to kill her.

item.title: Pogacar holds off Van der Poel to win Tour of Flanders but faces fine for running red light
item.description: Tadej Pogacar won a record-equalling third Tour of Flanders on Sunday after the world champion dropped his main rival Mathieu van der Poel with 18km to ride.

item.title: Fire-affected towns welcome Easter visitors
item.description: Holiday-makers have been welcomed back to fire-affected communities over Easter, as the long road to recovery continues.

item.title: Scattered rain, strong wind along Qld coast as forecasters watch TC Maila
item.description: Scattered rainfall is expected across much of the Queensland east coast this week.

item.title: Thousands in Texas protest against border wall through national park: ‘big love for Big Bend’
item.description: Thousands of people gathered at the steps of the Texas capitol on Saturday to protest against the construction of a border wall through Big Bend, in a show of bipartisan opposition to the White House’s plans.

item.title: Leeds hold nerve to win shootout after West Ham’s dramatic fightback
item.description: The familiar London Stadium rush for the exit has never been so misjudged.

item.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate
item.description: The 80-metre tower and small scattering of sheds perched on the cliffs of Tasmania's remote north-western edge seems fairly unassuming.

item.title: US intelligence says Chinese satellite imagery of bases is helping Iran
item.description: A Chinese company's publication of AI-enhanced satellite images of US bases in the Middle East is helping Iranian forces identify targets, US intelligence believes.

item.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree
item.description: Which is understandable, but perhaps also a little strange.

item.title: PSV crowned Eredivisie champions, Hearts drop points at Livingston and more – matchday live
item.description: Liverpool now do have the ball in the net, with a really quite brilliant improvised contorting backheel volley, but it’s ruled out for offside.

item.title: West Ham United v Leeds United: FA Cup – live
item.description: Both managers have picked but not full-strength XIs, with five changes for West Ham and three for Leeds.

item.title: Artemis's stunning Moon pictures - science or holiday photos?
item.description: Nasa is releasing a steady stream of stunning pictures of the Moon and Earth taken by the astronauts on its Artemis II Moon mission.

item.title: Trying to conceive? Welcome to the worry-filled world of ‘trimester zero’
item.description: An army of ‘pregnancy prep’ influencers is offering would-be parents everything from sensible advice to quackery and questionable supplements.

item.title: Gladiator Jodie Ounsley looks back: ‘There weren’t many girls like me at school. I always liked bashing into people’
item.description: Born in Dewsbury, West Yorkshire, in 2001, Jodie Ounsley is an athlete, television personality and author.

item.title: ‘We’re home’: Even in a draw, Inter Miami’s stadium opener was a win for David Beckham
item.description: Lionel Messi may have scored, captained the side and had a quarter of the new building named in his honor, but this was unmistakably Inter Miami co-owner Sir David Beckham’s night.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass
item.description: Thousands of worshippers flocked to St Peter's Square on Sunday to hear Pope Leo XIV deliver his first Easter Mass address as pontiff.

item.title: New AHA guidelines on healthy eating emphasize plants, olive oil; limit saturated fat
item.description: The American Heart Association is releasing dietary guidelines on the heels of confusing guidelines announced by the federal government.

item.title: How to make the perfect Portuguese feijoada – recipe | Felicity Cloake's How to make the perfect
item.description: If you are trying to incorporate more beans and pulses into your diet, as I am, then this robust, one-pot feast, which food writer Edite Vieira describes as “a marvellous standby of the Portuguese kitchen”, is one to bear in mind.

item.title: ‘I still think it’s one of the great films of all time’: All the President’s Men turns 50
item.description: The rustle of a notepad.

item.title: This is how we do it: ‘The fact he’s comfortable enough with his sexuality to be intimate with other men is so hot to me’
item.description: After my first marriage ended 15 years ago, I felt as if I had a ton of catching up to do when it came to sexual experiences.

item.title: ‘I was getting down with a guy and he decided to put on One Love. It was creepy’: Duncan James from Blue’s honest playlist
item.description: The Blue singer thinks Aqua deserve respect and his mum once did karaoke with a legend.

item.title: Sydney Kings break Adelaide 36ers hearts in overtime thriller to win NBL Championship
item.description: The Sydney Kings have won their sixth NBL title with a dramatic 113-101 overtime defeat of the Adelaide 36ers in the decisive fifth game of the championship series.

item.title: An AI bot invited me to its party in Manchester. It was a pretty good night
item.description: Two weeks ago, an AI bot invited me to a party it was organising in Manchester.

item.title: Welcome to the MrBeastification of British politics: the latest trick up Nigel Farage's sleeve | Kirsty Major
item.description: A man stands in the middle of a suburban English street holding a wad of cash in his hands.

item.title: Spurs gamble on creative but combative De Zerbi conjuring up an escape plan
item.description: It is ironic that the man who has appointed Roberto De Zerbi to be Tottenham manager, just as the club faces its most critical seven games this century, is also partially responsible for one of the most successful managerial recruitments in Mikel Arteta, albeit for north London rivals, Arsenal.

item.title: What we know so far about rescue of US airman in Iran
item.description: The US has rescued the missing crew member of the US F-15 fighter jet which was shot down on Friday over southern Iran.

item.title: 'I bought the theme park I loved growing up'
item.description: Owning a theme park is the stuff of dreams for some children, but for James Mellors, that dream has become a reality.

item.title: Is taking your cat for a walk good for them or just good for TikTok?
item.description: Roo has her harness on and her owner Alana Kestle has her lead in hand - the pair are ready to go out for a walk.

item.title: US F-15 airman rescued in Iran after special forces operation
item.description: US President Donald Trump has confirmed an airman of a fighter plane downed over Iran has been rescued.

item.title: Is the UK falling out of love with social media?
item.description: Posting significant events in your life, from birthdays to weddings and promotions, is a social media staple.

item.title: Australians find joy amid global uncertainty in Easter celebrations
item.description: At Easter Sunday services across the country, worshippers have been urged to look for joy and hope, despite the "heaviness" of recent global events.

item.title: Kings break NBL trophy after dramatic overtime win as 36ers blow late lead
item.description: The Sydney Kings have won their sixth NBL title with a dramatic 113-101 overtime defeat of the Adelaide 36ers in the decisive fifth game of the championship series.

item.title: Huge sea turtle rescued after being discovered stranded upside down
item.description: A large sea turtle weighing more than 50 kilograms has been rescued from a Urunga beach.

item.title: Travellers celebrate as major highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Cyclone Narelle dumped heavy rain causing significant damage to the North West Coastal Highway in the Pilbara.

item.title: Cambodia unveils statue to honour famous landmine-sniffing rat
item.description: A famous mine-clearing rat, who was awarded a gold medal for his heroism, has been commemorated with the world's first statue dedicated to a landmine-detecting rat.

item.title: 'Amazing' breakthrough as rare bird relearns its lost 'love song'
item.description: When wildlife photographer Richard Shaw spotted a rare regent honeyeater on the New South Wales Mid North Coast, he was not sure if his eyes were playing tricks on him.

item.title: Streetwear and crop tops take World Cup fashion to new heights
item.description: Shirt launches are a hotly anticipated part of every World Cup.

item.title: Brazilian admits to 'fan-girling' during win over Gilmore at Bells Beach
item.description: Luana Silva defeated Stephanie Gilmore in the first round at Bells Beach.

item.title: The man who became one of India's greatest stage queens
item.description: In mid-20th Century Bengal in eastern India, some of the biggest female stars on stage were actually men.

item.title: Archbishop to pray for Middle East peace in first Easter sermon
item.description: The Archbishop of Canterbury will call for an end to "violence and destruction" in the Middle East during her first Easter sermon as leader of the Church of England.

item.title: Power restored to many homes after Storm Dave brings high winds
item.description: Around 2,000 homes were left without power in Northern Ireland on Saturday after Storm Dave brought high winds.

item.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for 'Easter eggs' yields stunning images
item.description: Scott McCook hunts "eggs" from the air, but not the traditional Easter kind.

item.title: The moment I knew: he kissed me and it felt like I was standing on the edge of a whole new life
item.description: In the summer of 2014, I was living in Adelaide with my two young children in a very hot rental house with a low ceiling and a rat problem.

item.title: Friends, with recipes: how a love of food brought my husband and I together, and helped us part
item.description: For our first date – a picnic on the grassy banks of the Molonglo River near Canberra – the man I would marry brought the tartan blanket (tick), the wine (tick), a crusty baguette (tick), the cheese (tick), and then the ka-boom of a chicken and grape salad he’d made from a recipe in a gourmet magazine.

item.title: Thom Yorke's solo tour was one of a kind. This Aussie film captured it
item.description: Our social feeds are inundated with DIY concert footage, to the degree that artists and venues have banned the use of smartphones, and we typically know what to expect from the biggest overseas touring acts before they arrive on our shores.

item.title: European football: Real Mallorca hand Barcelona a gift with shock win over Real Madrid
item.description: Real Mallorca dealt a major blow to Real Madrid’s title aspirations as Vedat Muriqi struck an added-time winner to seal a shock 2-1 win for the hosts, leaving Real four points behind Barcelona before the leaders play Atlético Madrid later on Saturday.

item.title: What are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?
item.description: From influencers to athletes, high-profile figures are hailing peptides as the route to wellness, claiming they help with injury repair, weight loss, anti-ageing and mood.

item.title: A Fox host says ‘many people’ think women shouldn’t be president. Thank goodness we’ve got a man in charge | Arwa Mahdawi
item.description: Oh dear, it looks like Jesse Watters’ mother needs to give him a good talking to again.

item.title: Outspoken Iranians overseas say their loved ones are being detained back home
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Iran’s government is detaining family members and threatening to seize property of Iranian opposition figures in exile, some tell The Associated Press, in the latest crackdown on dissenting voices as the war rages on.

item.title: ‘Don’t wait four more years’: World Cup co-hosts Canada offer Italy fans free shirt swap
item.description: Italian football fans are being encouraged to put their national team’s World Cup 2026 qualification failure behind them – by backing co-hosts Canada instead.

item.title: Jared Kushner is seeking peace deals in the Middle East. He’s also raising money for his own firm | Mohamad Bazzi
item.description: After Donald Trump returned to the White House, his son-in-law and former senior adviser Jared Kushner declined to take a job in the new administration and instead planned to focus on running his Miami-based private equity firm.

item.title: ‘The shadows, the figures playing basketball … I waited for the magic to appear – then it did’: José Luis Morales Martín’s best phone picture
item.description: José Luis Morales Martín took this photo from his living room window on a hot September afternoon.

item.title: Sunday best: Thomasina Miers’ recipes for aromatic chicken one-pot and salted caramel banana cake
item.description: I love Mexican chillies for the subtle flavour they give to cooking.

item.title: Chess Mates: the fantastic true story of the sex toy rumour that buzzed around the world
item.description: Chess Mates (Netflix, Tuesday) has an unsettling early gambit: the face of Piers Morgan, looming via archive footage.

item.title: Swans complete huge win over Eagles as Heeney stars on return
item.description: Sydney has thumped West Coast by 128 points, sending the Eagles crashing back to earth at Perth Stadium, thanks to a four-goal performance from returning star Isaac Heeney.

item.title: ‘There’s more to life than work’: Bangkok’s young people embrace mass outdoor aerobics sessions
item.description: It’s evening rush hour in central Bangkok, the roads are clogged with traffic and the air is heavy from the heat.

item.title: Bowen says fuel supply 'strong', tells Easter travellers not to cancel plans
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: The most beautiful coast in the world? Exploring New Zealand’s overlooked Nelson Tasman by sand and sea
item.description: The visitor to New Zealand’s South Island knows what they have to see.

item.title: Demons star praised for reaching 'another level' with career-best form
item.description: Kysaiah Pickett has been one of Melbourne's best players during its opening three matches of the season.

item.title: After decades of success, love and loss, outback pub changes hands
item.description: Standing on the front deck of the watering hole he turned into an outback icon, Stephen "Burnsie" Burns leans on the shoulders of Tom Leggett.

item.title: Five Great Reads: When your mum is a cult leader, the OnlyFans inheritance, and 35,000 pints of stolen Guinness
item.description: Dear readers, this is my final instalment of Five Great Reads, but fear not – my wonderful colleague Imogen Dewey will be back in your inboxes next week and beyond.

item.title: Poo of 'endearing' bat worth hundreds of millions, study says
item.description: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed their poo could be worth hundreds-of-millions of dollars.

item.title: Cops would not have had 'luxury' of taking Dezi Freeman alive, experts say
item.description: A former police hostage negotiator says he was sure Dezi Freeman's seven-month run from the law "was going to end in tears" and that the fugitive would never surrender.

item.title: ‘Luckily, I wasn’t decapitated’: 17 comedians’ stories from 40 years of Melbourne international comedy festival
item.description: Colin Lane: Frank and I did our first MICF as The Found Objects [with Scott Casley].

item.title: Harry Kane, England’s Ballon d’Or hopeful, is finally getting the love he deserves | Barney Ronay
item.description: Everyone has their favourite mental comfort food, the stuff that makes you feel good in troubled times.

item.title: Five matches, 16 days, a season to save: Slot’s Liverpool vision faces a defining moment
item.description: There were more important reasons for Jürgen Klopp’s return to Liverpool last Saturday, but it was apposite his presence reminded Anfield of the unshakable bond and belief they once shared, the joy his football brought.

item.title: Raye tops album charts with This Music May Contain Hope
item.description: Award-winning singer-songwriter Raye has topped the album charts for the first time with This Music May Contain Hope.

item.title: Live: US crew member rescued after fighter jet downed over Iran
item.description: One US crew member has been found after an American fighter jet was shot down over Iran, a US official has confirmed.

item.title: Good Friday around the world – in pictures
item.description: Christians take part in processions and re-enactments to commemorate the crucifixion of Jesus Christ Continue reading.

item.title: EV adoption in America: Who's winning, who's losing?
item.description: Some OEMs saw double-digit growth in Q1, others saw double-digit declines.

item.title: Artemis II’s moon-bound astronauts capture Earth’s brilliant blue beauty as they leave it behind
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla.

item.title: Trainer 'never had a penny' on his 300-1 winning horse
item.description: The trainer of a horse which has equalled the UK racing record for longest-priced winner says he "never had a penny on" his winner.

item.title: Scrimp on moisturiser, splurge on serum: the secrets of a great skincare routine
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: My doctor said joy can be found in unexpected places. For me it was the Adelaide 36ers | Olivia De Zilva
item.description: I tried everything to find salvation in 2024.

item.title: 'You look beautiful': Artemis II crew makes contact from space – video
item.description: The four astronauts on Nasa's lunar mission have spoken about their experience of the journey so far via video.

item.title: 'Not pretending to be 20': U2 drop 'endearingly honest' second surprise EP
item.description: Irish rock legends U2 have released their second surprise EP of 2026, with the six-track Easter Lily coming just over 40 days after Days of Ash.

item.title: CalMac in 'better place' as third ferry returns after repairs
item.description: Scotland's ferry shortage is reaching a "much better place" with a third major vessel set to resume service, CalMac says.

item.title: Cocktail of the week: Acre’s fashionably late – recipe | The good mixer
item.description: Our cocktail list features both classics and new ones we’ve created ourselves, including this old fashioned of sorts.

item.title: ‘Taking my clothes off is my whole life!’ Bryan Cranston on the glorious gross-out return of Malcolm in the Middle
item.description: Did they? It’s been 20 years since the Emmy-winning sitcom about an outrageous working-class US family with the titular child genius went off air.

item.title: Oxford Brookes’ student racing team target Silverstone win
item.description: At the Oxford Brookes Headington campus, more than 100 students are busy building the fastest, best designed race car possible for this year’s Formula Student competition.

item.title: Easter eggs can be dyed and still eaten. Just follow these tips to make sure it’s safe
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D.

item.title: Culture of care: surreal celebrations of Iranian tenderness – in pictures
item.description: As the daughter of refugees, Iranian American artist Sheida Soleimani’s work reframes caring for bodies – both human and animal - as a political act.

item.title: Unhittable: are the modern era’s weightlifting, analytics-fueled pitchers too good?
item.description: In a 1940 publicity stunt, the Cleveland Indians’ flamethrowing pitcher, Bob Feller, tested which was faster: One of his own blazing deliveries, or a motorcycle.

item.title: Lizzo’s reinvention faces its greatest test yet
item.description: Lizzo’s ascent to mainstream stardom was the type of fairytale that pop dreams are made of.

item.title: 'Trust us, you look amazing': Artemis II astronauts speak from space
item.description: The Artemis II spacecraft has successfully made its way to the far side of the Moon after leaving the Earth's orbit.

item.title: 'The meaner we are, the better we get along' say Race Across the World siblings
item.description: When Katie's older brother Harrison told her he'd entered them into Race Across the World she wasn't filled with enthusiasm.

item.title: Helen Goh’s recipe for ricotta, rum and raisin cake | The sweet spot
item.description: This is a cake for the long, ambling tail-end of an Easter lunch.

item.title: Fresh autumn produce to make the most of in April
item.description: "A lot of people have fruit trees and you can literally see the seasons changing on the street," says Helly Raichura.

item.title: ‘I lost a $3m brand deal. I was like: OK, losers!’ Swedish pop provocateur Zara Larsson on fame, fun and fighting the power
item.description: On a warm spring day, Brooklyn’s century-old Paramount theatre has been transformed into a base camp for all things Zara Larsson.

item.title: Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win?
item.description: Hungarians go to the polls on 12 April in Europe’s most consequential election of the year, with Viktor Orbán, the country’s illiberal prime minister and global far-right icon, facing possible defeat, after 16 years in power, by a former loyalist, Péter Magyar.

item.title: Packed to the gills: shoppers embrace new Sydney Fish Market during Good Friday rush
item.description: “I just love crustaceans!” one eager hunter declares as they stand shoulder to shoulder with other prawn pursuers passing through Sydney Fish Market on Good Friday morning, the lunch rush not even yet under way.

item.title: Live: Voss on hot seat as Blues tackle Kangaroos to kick off Good Friday
item.description: Carlton faces North Melbourne in what is a must-win clash for under-fire senior coach Michael Voss.

item.title: Alana King spins Australia to crushing win over West Indies to seal ODI series sweep
item.description: Alana King has spun Australia to a commanding nine-wicket victory over West Indies as the tourists completed an ODI series sweep in St Kitts.

item.title: Cyclones, culture and heroes are among WA's top photo picks
item.description: From the13-year-old who garnered international attention for his heroic efforts to rescue his family to a unique severe tropical cyclone, here some of the photos that illustrated our news coverage in Western Australia during February, the last month of summer, and March, the first month of autumn.

item.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know
item.description: A rare water-borne parasite likely spread on Darwin's sodden wet season playing fields is behind an outbreak of severe conjunctivitis that has so far affected more than 100 people.

item.title: 122-year-old yacht built in UK brewery chasing win as it races Brisbane to Gladstone
item.description: The 78th annual Brisbane to Gladstone Yacht Race begins today.

item.title: What's so cool about the far side of the moon? A NASA Artemis II scientist explains
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with NASA's Kelly Evans Young, the Artemis science flight operations lead, about the rigors of space and the lunar slingshot to get home.

item.title: UConn star sophomore Sarah Strong named the AP Player of the Year in women’s college basketball
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Sarah Strong of UConn was named The Associated Press women’s basketball Player of the Year on Thursday after leading the Huskies to an undefeated season, setting the stage for a run to the Final Four.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A lost penguin, successful NASA rocket launch and address to the nation
item.description: Take a squiz at the ABC's New Quiz topic page and see if you're a whiz at our previous quizzes.

item.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
item.description: A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to.

item.title: 'Unbelievable!': Backshall overjoyed at rare sighting of orcas
item.description: Wildlife explorer Steve Backshall was overjoyed to have spotted two of the UK's last surviving orcas as he described them as "international celebrities".

item.title: HBO to air standalone special on the making of new Harry Potter series
item.description: HBO has more Harry Potter magic up its sleeve – today, the company announced a standalone, behind-the-scenes special to accompany its upcoming TV adaptation of Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone.

item.title: William hails 'incredible' £20m milestone for Bowelbabe fund
item.description: A cancer research fund set up by Dame Deborah James has been hailed as an "incredible milestone" by the Prince of Wales after it hit the £20m mark.

item.title: Puppy love: why asking my boyfriend to coparent Basil the greyhound was the most important proposal of all | Patrick Lenton
item.description: Recently I got down on one knee and presented my boyfriend with some jewellery, and asked if he would commit to caring for a very long, cute, stinky boy.

item.title: Peach PRC on stardom, synaesthesia and sexuality: ‘There was so much joy and fun in stripping’
item.description: Having lived much of her adult life online, Shaylee Curnow – better known as Peach PRC (or just Peach) – is now in her back-to-nature era.

item.title: 'Stay Alive,' about daily life in Nazi Berlin, shows how easy it is to just go along
item.description: It's been 80 years since Adolf Hitler shot himself in his bunker, yet our fascination with the Nazi era seems eternal.

item.title: Scientists excited to receive first Artemis signal
item.description: Scientists at a satellite earth station in Cornwall were excited to receive their first signal from Nasa's Artemis II mission to the Moon.

item.title: What spring festivals remind us about food, family and fresh starts
item.description: Spring has a way of bringing us together.

item.title: Why thousands of New Yorkers swap gas for induction stoves in clean energy push: ‘It makes sense’
item.description: Marcos Ramos hasn’t been able to cook a full meal at home in nearly four years, after a gas leak resulted in a lengthy supply cut off for his New York City apartment building.

item.title: Scientists' efforts to free whale stuck in the Baltic Sea cause online firestorm
item.description: German scientists face a social media firestorm as they try to save a humpback whale in the Baltic Sea.

item.title: 'Oh my goodness, that is spectacular': See BBC science editor react to launch
item.description: Nasa's Artemis II spacecraft has launched and is now orbiting Earth, where it will then head on to circle the Moon as part of a 10-day mission.

item.title: Moira Deeming to secure top spot on Victorian Liberal ticket
item.description: Moira Deeming will secure a top spot on the Victorian Liberal party’s upper house ticket unopposed – less than a week after members voted to dump her – after the withdrawal of candidates from a re-run ballot.

item.title: Wee collected at festivals could help with a project to grow a new forest
item.description: Scientists behind a pioneering project to turn human wee into plant fertiliser are hoping to grow their first forest.

item.title: One Nation wins fourth seat in SA – as it happened
item.description: One Nation has picked up its fourth lower house seat in the South Australian election.

item.title: Tszyu ready to revive world title dream with Easter Sunday bout
item.description: Tim Tszyu fights undefeated Albanian Denis Nurja in Wollongong on Easter Sunday.

item.title: Families praise charity for Easter holiday support
item.description: Parents have praised a charity which is helping to support families with the cost of living over the school Easter holidays.

item.title: ‘Walking is the best way to discover offbeat Corfu’: a spring hike across the Greek island
item.description: The riverside was heaving.

item.title: Björk, Rihanna and a passionate embrace: visions of love – in pictures
item.description: A new book celebrating four decades of fashion photography duo Inez and Vinoodh features celebrity portraits, surrealist visions and a meditation on love itself Continue reading.

item.title: Call the Midwife praised by one of the nuns who inspired it
item.description: After a dramatic end to the 15th series of Call the Midwife and with work beginning on a prequel to the TV drama, one of the nuns who inspired the series has been praising its attention to detail.

item.title: The PM said 'enjoy your Easter'. Here's what you should know first about fuel
item.description: As Easter begins, many travellers will set out across Australia to enjoy their long weekend.

item.title: Thursday news quiz: daring dogs, delinquent capybaras and far too many bananas
item.description: Test yourself on topical news trivia, pop culture and general knowledge every Thursday.

item.title: Endo dreams of sushi: a trip around Japan with one of the world’s greatest chefs
item.description: Endo Kazutoshi was on the train to Paris when he heard about the fire.

item.title: Where can I buy food and alcohol in Australia on Good Friday?
item.description: Good Friday may be steeped in Christian tradition, but in Australia it comes with a custom of its own – the national scramble to find open food and alcohol shops.

item.title: Shark relocation offers temporary safety boost, Queensland study suggests
item.description: New research suggests relocating tiger sharks away from swimming beaches could temporarily reduce risk of shark bites.

item.title: Here is the key to getting through The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
item.description: Ask any Nintendo fan worth their salt and they'll tell you legendary video game designer and Mario Bros.

item.title: Safety work means no Easter opening for iconic WA tree climb
item.description: Southern WA's landmark Gloucester Tree climb will not reopen in time for Easter.

item.title: DHS boss rescinds restrictive $100,000 approval process, giving hope to FEMA relief efforts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin on Wednesday rescinded a rule that DHS expenditures over $100,000 be personally approved by his office, ending a widely criticized policy implemented by his predecessor Kristi Noem that critics said put a particular burden on the Federal Emergency Management Agency ’s work aiding disaster response and recovery.

item.title: 'We entered Race Across the World to honour dying wish'
item.description: When Margo Oakley, now 59, was introduced to her older sister's new boyfriend her first impressions of the "po-faced" and "judgey" young man were not great.

item.title: Live: 'We have a beautiful moon rise': NASA's Artemis II crew headed for the lunar orbit
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission lifted-off at 9:35am AEDT, with the space agency hoping to send humans around the Moon for the first time since 1972.

item.title: Guardian power rankings: France lead the way with Senegal and Japan in top 10
item.description: Some countries, like Kosovo and Iraq, have only just qualified after playoffs while most have been fine-tuning in friendlies“There’s more talent and potential than in 2022,” Kylian Mbappé said ominously this week after France had beaten Brazil 2-1 despite having Dayot Upamecano sent off after 55 minutes.

item.title: The FDA approves a new obesity pill, giving patients another option
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has approved the second GLP-1 pill to treat obesity, this time from drugmaker Eli Lilly.

item.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
item.description: In the ongoing war with the United States and Israel, Iran has been threatening to mine the vital Strait of Hormuz, with reports suggesting it may have already placed devices in the narrow shipping chokepoint.

item.title: What Australia can learn from French cities that made public transport free
item.description: A year after the last energy crisis shook the world, a French city made a bold move.

item.title: I grew up the golden child before becoming the black sheep. I’m much happier now
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Good Friday is a unique and solemn day for Christians, with ancient prayers and fervent processions
item.description: Celebrated the Friday before Easter, Good Friday commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus.

item.title: FDA grants speedy approval to Eli Lilly’s weight-loss pill for obesity
item.description: Federal regulators on Wednesday approved Eli Lilly’s new weight-loss pill, a second daily oral medication to treat obesity and other weight-related conditions.

item.title: Macron praises Europe’s predictability in face of countries that ‘hurt you without even informing you’ – Europe live
item.description: Meanwhile, French president Emmanuel Macron praised Europe’s “predictability” during a visit to Japan, contrasting it with countries that “could hurt you without even informing you” in an apparent swipe at Donald Trump, AFP reported.

item.title: Apple at 50 quiz: top sellers, turkeys and turtlenecks
item.description: How much do you know about the history of one of the most powerful computing companies on the planet?.

item.title: A truckload of F1 KitKats, a painting of fish: what is it that makes heists so delicious? | Imogen West-Knights
item.description: Now, let me begin by saying: stealing is bad.

item.title: How to wear a quarter-zip jumper without looking like a finance bro (and 14 of the best)
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.
